layout.rationale_one_line: Universally adopted post-Control. V1's full_screen_dark is confounded; no reason to adopt dark variant without cleaner evidence.

citation.verbatim_quote: Bottom-modal popup interrupts and is dismissed before read. 31/50 users. Resolved in V1-V4.

modal_interrupt.rationale_one_line: 62% cross-segment friction on modal; every post-Control variant removed it.

citation.ref: matrix.variants.Control/V1/V4 use 'none'; V2/V3 use crown_header which is confounded with 22% CTA-visibility friction
citation.verbatim_quote: Low-contrast CTA missed entirely; banner mistapped. 11/50 (22%). V2-specific.

branding.rationale_one_line: Crown header is confounded with V2/V3 cluster; safer without, and V4's performance backs this choice directionally.

citation.verbatim_quote: No price shown on activation; Skeptical Investors assume hidden charge. 39/50 (78%). Resolved in V1-V4.

price_visibility.rationale_one_line: Not opaque (78% friction resolved). visible_primary is cleanly separable from refund copy — unlike visible_with_framing which is confounded in V2/V3.

citation.verbatim_quote: Proposed honest framing for V4's dual-CTA mismatch. Pairs with trade_evidence=real_closed_trade — they share the 'show a real thing' honesty mechanism.

cta_primary_label.rationale_one_line: V4's 33% mismatch-notice rate is a trust cost ('mildly deceptive'); honest label paired with real trade delivery removes the framing gap.

citation.verbatim_quote: Only cta_style changed. Bargain Hunter +16pts (46->62). Trust Seeker -10pts (50->40). Skeptical +9pts (8->17). Curious +7pts (20->27). Weighted +6.42pt at default audience.

cta_style.rationale_one_line: Only clean-contrast-derived pts score in the dataset. Net +6.42pt despite Trust Seeker -10pt penalty. Alternative 'muted_premium' (untested) explored in flags.

alternative_for_exploratory_mode.expected_mechanism: Preserve +16/+7/+9 pt lift for Bargain/Curious/Skeptical while removing the -10 Trust Seeker premium-feel penalty. Untested.
alternative_for_exploratory_mode.risk: Mechanism may not transfer; untested combined with 2 other untested values increases stacking risk.

citation.verbatim_quote: Outline 'Unlock FREE trade' vs sticky '₹1 Trial' perceived as label mismatch / mildly deceptive. 5/50 (10%) flagged; 33% of V4 users noticed the discrepancy (element-level).

cta_stack.rationale_one_line: V4's dual stack introduced a 33%-noticed mismatch. Reverting to single CTA removes the trust cost; upside of dual was confounded in V3->V4 (5-way change).

citation.verbatim_quote: Countdown timer perceived as manufactured urgency, alienates Skeptical Investors. 6/12 (50% of segment). V1 only; removed in V2-V4.

urgency_mechanism.rationale_one_line: 50% of Skeptical Investors flagged V1's timer. V2-V4 removed it. All untested alternatives (scarcity, social-proof-realtime) inherit the same manipulative-urgency family.

citation.verbatim_quote: Proposed: 'Refund in 60s to source. No questions.' Closes the Skeptical Investor trust gap with concrete SLA. Pairs with trade_evidence=real_closed_trade (both are concreteness mechanisms for Skeptical). 64% of users cited the refund clause as strongest pricing reassurance (element-level).

refund_or_guarantee_copy.rationale_one_line: 64% element-level evidence that refund messaging is the strongest pricing reassurance; making it explicit with an SLA strengthens the mechanism.
refund_or_guarantee_copy.dependency_flag: V4.refund_or_guarantee_copy is source-tagged absent but a V4 Skeptical quote mentions 'refund' — if V4 actually has refund copy, this choice is more defensible (known-safe upgrade to existing copy); if not, it's an untested addition.

citation.verbatim_quote: V1 peaked Trust Seeker at 60% (highest in dataset) and Curious Beginner at 27%. V1 was the only variant with regulatory_plus_evidence; dropping it in V2-V4 produced the 8/50 (16%) 'abstract_metrics_vs_named_wins' persistent friction.

trust_signal.rationale_one_line: Observationally strongest combination for Trust Seeker (60% vs V4's 50%) and Curious Beginner. Attribution confounded with evidence_detail but the V1 evidence is substantial.

citation.ref: matrix.variants.V1 + matrix.citations['I bought it last year...']
citation.verbatim_quote: 71% of V1 users cited a stock by name. Curious Beginner anchor: 'I bought it last year — if they nailed that, I should listen' (V1 ZOMATO win).

evidence_detail.rationale_one_line: Restores the Curious Beginner named-stock anchor that was load-bearing in V1. Alternative 'real_outcome_disclosure' (untested) is a stronger Skeptical mechanism but conflicts visually with trade_evidence=real_closed_trade which already provides disclosure.

citation.verbatim_quote: Blurred trade card reads as a gimmick to Skeptical Investors. 12/12 (100% of segment). Persistent across V2/V3/V4. Supporting quote: 'Show me a real trade or don't'. Overlay proposal: real_closed_trade (fully disclosed entry/exit/days/gain) removes the trust gap entirely.

trade_evidence.rationale_one_line: 100% Skeptical flag rate on blurred_card is the highest single-segment friction in the dataset. real_closed_trade is the only candidate with a mechanism that removes it.

applied_rules.rule: if price_visibility=visible_with_framing then refund_or_guarantee_copy shares the banner (same UI element)
applied_rules.resolution: Not triggered — chose visible_primary, which decouples from refund copy. Refund SLA lives as a standalone line below the CTA.

applied_rules.rule: if trust_signal=regulatory_plus_evidence then evidence_detail must be non-none
if cta_stack=dual, cta labels must not create a mismatch (V4 friction)
applied_rules.resolution: Not triggered — chose single; mismatch mechanism eliminated.
Not triggered — chose real_closed_trade which removes the mechanism.
Triggered. Already reflected in per_segment_impact; no additional penalty. Alternative (muted_premium, untested) flagged for exploratory mode.

conflicts_surfaced_and_resolved.conflict: evidence_detail=named_past_outcome (picked) vs real_outcome_disclosure (untested alternative that's stronger for Skeptical)
conflicts_surfaced_and_resolved.resolution: Picked named_past_outcome because trade_evidence=real_closed_trade already provides the full disclosure. Doubling up would be redundant and cluttered. Named wins (carousel) + one real closed trade = two evidence formats, different audiences, non-overlapping.

untested_stack.note: 3 untested values within the ≤4 threshold. Each has a named mechanism in the overlay. Non-linearity discount applied to predictions (factor ~0.7).

drivers: trade_evidence: blurred_card → real_closed_trade removes 100%-flagged trust gap (strongest driver)
refund_or_guarantee_copy: absent → explicit_sla closes 64%-cited refund-reassurance gap
trust_signal + evidence_detail: implicit+none → regulatory_plus_evidence + named_past_outcome (V1 Skeptical was 17% vs V4's 25%, so the trust lift mostly stacks with V4's other gains)
cta_primary_label: 'Unlock FREE trade' → 'See 1 real trade, free' removes 33%-noticed label mismatch
failure_conditions: real_closed_trade reads as cherry-picking ('they disclosed ONE trade — what about the rest?'), replacing blurred-card-gimmick suspicion with cherry-pick suspicion
'Refund in 60s' SLA is not operationally true, creating a bigger trust violation than the prior absence of the copy
The honest label makes the ₹1 expectation-gap worse, not better (user expects 'actually free' and finds ₹1 step)

drivers: evidence_detail: named_past_outcome restored (71% stock-naming signal in V1; ZOMATO-anchor mechanism: 'I bought it last year — if they nailed that, I should listen')
failure_conditions: Named stocks in V5's carousel don't resonate with THIS Curious Beginner cohort — stock selection is out-of-matrix context that matters for this mechanism
real_closed_trade displaces the named_past_outcome carousel visually, and the anchor mechanism fails without visual prominence

drivers: cta_style=high_contrast_green preserved from V4 (+16pt in V2→V3 contrast — the primary Bargain driver)
other V5 changes are trust/evidence-oriented, neutral for Bargain Hunter
failure_conditions: V4 Bargain was already near ceiling (conversion avg 7s). Adding real_closed_trade + named wins carousel may slow the decision flow and cost 1-2pt through cognitive load
Dual-stack removal removes the 'two options' psychological hook that may have been part of V4's Bargain draw

drivers: trust_signal + evidence_detail restored (V1 Trust was 60% with these; V4 Trust = 50%; restoring should lift 3-8pt)
cta_style=high_contrast_green retains the -10pt penalty observed in V2→V3 contrast — the primary Trust drag
failure_conditions: Restored trust signals don't offset the green CTA penalty — synthesizer should consider muted_premium (untested) for Trust-heavy audiences
Trust Seekers saw SEBI + named wins already in V1 — fatigue effect means V5 needs something new to get back to V1's 60%

Small sample: Trust Seeker n=10, so the -10pt V2→V3 penalty has a wide binomial interval; the true penalty could be less severe (in which case green is fine) or more severe (in which case muted_premium is necessary)

weighted_overall_prediction.confidence_diagnosis: 4 dimensions observed-high (modal_interrupt, price_visibility, cta_style, urgency_mechanism) / 5 observed-medium (layout, branding, cta_stack, trust_signal, evidence_detail) / 3 untested (cta_primary_label, refund_or_guarantee_copy, trade_evidence). Within the medium-confidence roll-up threshold. Non-linearity discount (~0.7) applied to simultaneous-change predictions.

exploratory_alternative.risk: Fourth untested value stacked on top of the existing three. Expected-mechanism argument is concrete (preserves Bargain's visual hierarchy while removing Trust's green-penalty mechanism) but has no observation in the dataset. Recommended only if audience skews Trust-heavy or if the engagement explicitly wants to showcase the system's ability to reach for untested upside.

flags: V5 predicted median 49.3% vs IDEA.md's 52-55% headline. The gap is explained by: (a) balanced-mode default chose green over muted_premium (-1pt); (b) this synthesis applies a non-linearity discount for 6 simultaneous V4→V5 changes. IDEA.md's 52-55% is the 'everything works as mechanism predicts' upper bound; my 53% high-tier matches that.

3 untested values stacked (cta_primary_label, refund_or_guarantee_copy, trade_evidence). Each has a strong named mechanism, but the combined untested interaction is unobserved. Post-ship evaluation should record predicted vs actual per-segment to close this loop.

V4.refund_or_guarantee_copy source inconsistency (matrix.flags item 2) carries forward: if V4 actually shows refund copy, explicit_sla is a known-safe upgrade; if not, it's adding a new element. Confidence on that dimension is conditional on source resolution.

Trust Seeker n=10 means the -10pt green penalty has a wide binomial interval. The balanced choice of green is defensible given the noise; the exploratory alternative (muted_premium) hedges against the penalty being at the high end of the interval.

handoff.next_step: adversary agent (IDEA.md Day 5) — must challenge these 12 choices before spec-writer runs.
handoff.adversary_input: this file + the matrix + the overlay
handoff.adversary_prompt_hint: Do not give general concerns. For each element choice, name the single failure mechanism most likely to make the post-ship actual underperform the prediction, and state the falsifiable prediction that would prove the objection right within 30 days of ship.
